hero.name: Le Minh Triet
hero.title: Backend Developer
hero.description: Crafting resilient and scalable backend systems to power modern applications.

about.title: About me
about.description: I am a passionate software engineer specializing in backend development. I love building robust architectures, optimizing databases, and setting up reliable infrastructure for high-performance applications.

projects.title: Food Delivery App
projects.shortDescription: A highly scalable microservices architecture for real-time food ordering.

detail.overview: Built a scalable food delivery application handling thousands of concurrent users. Improved order processing speed by 40% using Redis caching and message queues.
detail.architecture: The system utilizes a microservices architecture built with Node.js and Go. Communication between services is handled via RabbitMQ. Data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Redis acting as a caching layer for high-read requests.
detail.challenges: Handling peak mealtime traffic spikes without degrading performance. Solved by implementing dynamic auto-scaling and optimizing database queries using materialized views.

projects.title: Coral Spiral Abstract
projects.shortDescription: An algorithmic visualizer built for large dataset rendering.

detail.overview: Developed a high-performance backend to stream and sequence massive geometric datasets for frontend visualization. The system leverages WebGL for rendering and Python for batch processing.
detail.architecture: A FastAPI server manages WebSockets to broadcast geometric state changes to connected clients. Heavy computations are offloaded to worker nodes running optimized Rust modules.
detail.challenges: Reducing the payload size for complex geometries. Solved by implementing a custom binary serialization format, reducing data transfer size by 75%.

blog.title: Scaling Node.js for High Traffic Applications
blog.summary: Exploring clustering, worker threads, and microservices to maximize Node.js throughput.

blog.title: Advanced Database Indexing Strategies
blog.summary: How to optimize queries and avoid common pitfalls when creating indexes in PostgreSQL.
